title: modesciadium involucratum
intro: Modesciadium involucratum, also known as Desert Biscuit Root, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carrot family and is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Modesciadium involucratum include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of ailments, including colds, flu, and digestive disorders.
